name: ETH Balance Reclaim Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: deeper value reclaim that resumes into a modest continuation leg
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether a deeper pullback into value can reclaim balance and still produce a modest continuation leg, without depending on shallow trend-touch behavior. The edge is in repaired continuation after value is retaken.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim-continuation remains too sparse, if reclaims only bounce back to value without a second leg, or if the continuation component adds no value beyond plain mean reversion.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back deeper into value, repairs back through balance, and extends just enough for a second-leg continuation before chop reasserts.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a fully choppy 4h tape, shallow trend continuation is unreliable, but deeper repairs back into value can still generate a modest continuation burst before rotation resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is appropriate because value reclaims are visible and less noisy than on faster or thinner lanes.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains pure chop, the reclaim may stop at value and fail to produce the continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If reclaims trade with density but continuation is weak, convert the thesis into a pure reclaim mean-reversion management structure instead of forcing persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Reasonable signal count with many smaller wins and a minority of cleaner second-leg continuations.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h plus one confirmation lane show credible density and post-cost extension beyond simple value retake.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade output, or reclaims that consistently die at the first balance touch.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: controlled by broad reclaim logic rather than exact swing choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best clean lane for deeper reclaim-then-continue behavior in current conditions.
validation_basket.continue_if: Reclaims show real density and continuation beyond the first balance retake.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The second leg never appears or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent value repairs with modest but tradable continuation legs.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism is structural across majors rather than ETH-specific.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows similar reclaim-continuation behavior with comparable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only ETH works and the mechanism vanishes on BTC.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner, slightly less explosive version of the same reclaim pattern.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism survives faster structure without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h adds density without destroying trade quality.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: 1h becomes pure noise or requires cosmetic tightening.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller reclaim-continuation events than 4h.

long: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
